answer:
The Vonage Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers allow AI agents to discover and utilize Vonage’s developer documentation and communication APIs as tools [1][2]. These servers act as intermediaries, translating natural language requests into API calls for services like SMS, voice, and WhatsApp [2][3].

Vonage offers two primary servers:

The Documentation MCP Server provides AI assistants access to API references, code snippets, and tutorials [4][5]. It is hosted at:
`https://documentation-mcp.vonage.dev/mcp` [5]

The Server API Bindings (Tooling) MCP Server enables agents to perform actions such as managing applications, checking balances, and sending messages [4][6]. It is installed as an NPM package via `npx` [3][6].

Minimal configuration example for the Tooling Server:

```json
"vonage-tooling": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y", "@vonage/vonage-mcp-server-api-bindings"],
  "env": {
    "VONAGE_API_KEY": "your_key",
    "VONAGE_API_SECRET": "your_secret"
  }
}
```

These servers work with any MCP-compatible environment, including Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code [4][3].
